Avrora Karima.description: Remontant Italian variety (Karima). Early, high-yielding, transportable.
Avrora Karima.maturity: Early remontant. From May to October in waves.
Avrora Karima.color_and_shape: Berries 40-60 g, elongated-conical, bright red, glossy. Flesh dense, juicy, sweet.
Avrora Karima.farming: Yield up to 2 kg per bush. Good keeping quality.

Ada.description: Remontant German variety. Extended fruiting, up to 1 kg per bush per wave.
Ada.color_and_shape: Berries bright red, medium, sweet with sourness, strawberry aroma.
Ada.farming: Not suitable for commercial use (loose flesh). Requires covering below -20°C. Moderate runners.
Ada.yield: up to 1 kg per bush per wave

Admiral.description: Early universal-purpose variety. Uniform ripening. Bush vigorous, tall.
Admiral.color_and_shape: Berries up to 40 g, firm juicy flesh, sweet with sourness taste.

Alexandrina.description: Remontant runnerless alpine strawberry. Fruits until snow. Unpretentious, drought-resistant, winter-hardy.
Alexandrina.maturity: Remontant, runnerless. From June until frost.
Alexandrina.color_and_shape: Berries 9-30 g, round, slightly pubescent, like wild strawberry.
Alexandrina.farming: Ideal for open ground, partial shade, balconies. Bush vigorous, semi-spreading.

Alina.description: Italian day-neutral remontant variety. High-yielding, long-lasting.
Alina.maturity: Day-neutral. From late May until frost.
Alina.color_and_shape: Berries 30-50 g, cone-shaped, dark red with orange tint. Flesh dense, very sweet, aromatic.
Alina.farming: Yield up to 3 kg per bush. Excellent transportability. Ideal for commercial growing.

Alisa.description: Mid-season variety, good taste and disease resistance.
Alisa.color_and_shape: Berries ~16 g, blunt-conical, dark red, glossy. Flesh dark red, sweet-sour.
Alisa.farming: Universal. Good transportability, medium winter hardiness.

Allegro.description: Early Dutch variety, resistant to spring frosts, high sugar concentration.
Allegro.color_and_shape: Berries 20-30 g, light red, very shiny, evenly conical. Flesh sweet, dense.
Allegro.farming: High yield, outpaces many early varieties.

Alba.description: Very early Italian variety, known for high yield and uniform ripening. Vigorous bush.
Alba.maturity: Very early, non-remontant. Fruiting from late May.
Alba.color_and_shape: Berries 25-30 g (up to 45 g), elongated-conical, bright red, glossy.
Alba.farming: Winter-hardy, resistant to return frosts. Undemanding in care.

Albion.description: Popular remontant American variety for commercial use. Vigorous bush, upright flower stalks.
Albion.maturity: Remontant (DND). 3-4 waves from June to October.
Albion.color_and_shape: Berries 30-50 g, dark red, glossy, dense flesh. Sweet taste.
Albion.farming: Yield up to 3 kg per bush. Disease resistant, excellent commercial qualities.

Alpine Giant.description: Ultra-early alpine strawberry variety. Compact bushes, can be grown indoors.
Alpine Giant.color_and_shape: Berries up to 15-18 g, conical, red or dark cherry. Flesh dense, juicy, dessert taste with sourness, strong aroma.
Alpine Giant.farming: Winter-hardy, productive. Suitable for commercial growing.

Altess.description: Day-neutral remontant variety. Very productive, tasty, aromatic, does not get sick.
Altess.color_and_shape: Large berries, sweet with aroma when fully ripe.
Altess.farming: High-yielding. Bushes look better than many.

Alturas.description: Early remontant variety (Alturas). High yield, large fruits, strong bushes.
Alturas.origin: USA (C. Langenberg, D. Shaw, C. Larsen)
Alturas.maturity: Early remontant (DND). From May to October.
Alturas.color_and_shape: Large berries, lighter color, good taste.
Alturas.farming: Yield up to 3-4 kg per bush.

Amandine.description: New for 2024, French DND variety. Very productive (more than 1 kg per bush).

Amarant.description: Late-season Polish variety (Niwa Amarant). High yield, winter hardiness, drought resistance.
Amarant.farming: Resistant to diseases and strawberry mite.

Amulet.description: Russian variety, adapted to low temperatures (-25°C), strong immunity. Compact bushes, few runners.
Amulet.farming: Yield up to 2 kg per bush. Can produce second crop in autumn.

Anais.description: Remontant French variety, descendant of Mara des Bois. High-yielding, winter-hardy, disease resistant.
Anais.color_and_shape: Uniform berries, exquisite taste, strong wild strawberry aroma.
Anais.farming: Vigorous, compact bush. Long fruiting period.

Pineberry (Ananasnaya).description: Unique variety with pineapple flavor. White berries with red seeds. Bushes up to 25 cm.
Pineberry (Ananasnaya).color_and_shape: Small berries (15-25 mm), from green to white with red seeds.
Pineberry (Ananasnaya).farming: Low-yielding, but disease resistant. Synonyms: White Dream, White Pineapple.

Anastasia.description: Universal variety from crossing Redgauntlet and Festivalnaya. Vigorous, upright bush.
Anastasia.color_and_shape: Berries 7-16 g, blunt-conical, red, shiny, slightly bumpy. Flesh red, medium density, sweet-sour, aromatic.

Anita.description: Very early Italian variety, ripens earlier than Alba. Vigorous bush.
Anita.color_and_shape: Berries 30-45 g, bright red, sweet with peach aftertaste. Good transportability.

Anteya.description: Early remontant Italian variety, runnerless. Medium bush.
Anteya.color_and_shape: Berries 35-40 g, conical or comb-shaped, bright red with shine. Flesh dense, red, very aromatic and sweet (4.7 points).

Aprica.description: Variety with unusual apricot-colored berries. Sweet taste with peach notes.
Aprica.color_and_shape: Berries up to 30 g, round-conical, yellow-orange. Tender flesh.

Arabella.description: Ornamental-fruit variety with pink flowers and sweet berries. Suitable for hanging baskets.
Arabella.color_and_shape: Berries 15-20 g, conical, bright red. Sweet taste with aroma.
Arabella.farming: Produces many runners. Looks good in hanging baskets.

Argentea.description: Large-fruited variety from Argentina. Resistant to heat and drought.
Argentea.color_and_shape: Berries 25-35 g, round, red. Sweet flesh.
Argentea.farming: Requires watering during drought. Medium winter hardiness.

Arianna.description: Early remontant variety. Compact bush, abundant fruiting.
Arianna.color_and_shape: Berries 20-30 g, conical, bright red, shiny. Dessert taste.

Arosa.description: French variety, known for high yield and dense berries.
Arosa.color_and_shape: Berries 30-40 g, wide-conical, orange-red. Dense, aromatic flesh.
Arosa.farming: Excellent transportability. Resistant to powdery mildew.

Aromas.description: Remontant American variety. Vigorous bush, moderate runners.
Aromas.color_and_shape: Berries 25-35 g, conical, dark red. Dense flesh, sweet with sourness.

Aroma Leta.description: Early variety with strong strawberry aroma. Medium bush.
Aroma Leta.color_and_shape: Berries 15-20 g, round, red. Juicy, aromatic flesh.

Auge.color_and_shape: Berries up to 50 g, conical, bright red. Dense, sweet flesh.

Asia.description: Mid-early Italian variety, one of the best dessert varieties. Voluminous, spreading bush.
Asia.color_and_shape: Berries 30-40 g (up to 50 g), cone-shaped, bright red, glossy, with yellow seeds. Sweet with sourness taste, strong aroma.

Baron Solemacher.description: Old German remontant alpine strawberry variety. Runnerless, very aromatic.
Baron Solemacher.maturity: Remontant, runnerless. From June until frost.
Baron Solemacher.color_and_shape: Berries 5-10 g, elongated, dark red. Sweet taste with strong aroma.
Baron Solemacher.farming: Ideal for borders and pots. Average yield.

Bounty.description: Variety with very sweet berries, reminiscent of Bounty (coconut).
Bounty.color_and_shape: Berries 20-30 g, round, light red. Tender, sweet flesh.

Belaya Dusha.description: White-fruited variety with pineapple flavor. White berries with red seeds.
Belaya Dusha.color_and_shape: Berries up to 15 g, round, white. Sweet, fruity taste.
Belaya Dusha.farming: Suitable for growing in partial shade. Average yield.

Belosnezhka.description: Remontant white strawberry. Compact bush, few runners.
Belosnezhka.color_and_shape: Berries 10-15 g, conical, white with pink blush. Sweet, strawberry taste.

Belrubi.description: Strawberry and garden strawberry hybrid (Zemklunika). Vigorous bush, winter-hardy.
Belrubi.color_and_shape: Berries 20-25 g, round, dark red. Sweet taste with spicy aroma.

Beltrans.description: Late variety for long storage. Dense berries.
Beltrans.color_and_shape: Berries 25-35 g, conical, dark red. Very dense, sweet flesh.
Beltrans.farming: Well suited for freezing. Shelf life up to 10 days.

Bely Lotus.description: Exotic white-fruited variety with melon aroma. Medium bush.
Bely Lotus.color_and_shape: Berries 20-25 g, round, white with pink seeds. Sweet, melon taste.
Bely Lotus.farming: Requires protection from bright sun. Average yield.

Bely Shved.description: White-fruited Swedish variety. Sweet berries, no sourness.
Bely Shved.color_and_shape: Berries 15-20 g, conical, white with pinkish tint. Tender flesh.
Bely Shved.farming: Frost resistant. Grows well in partial shade.

Big Boy.description: Large-fruited American variety. Name translates to 'big guy'.
Big Boy.color_and_shape: Berries up to 50 g, round-conical, red. Dense, sweet flesh.

Bliznetsy.description: Variety with frequent formation of twin berries. Compact bush.
Bliznetsy.color_and_shape: Berries 20-25 g, often fused in pairs, bright red. Sweet-sour taste.

Bogema.description: Variety with dark cherry berries, almost black. Dessert taste.
Bogema.color_and_shape: Berries 30-40 g, round, dark. Dense flesh, sweet with spicy aroma.

Bogota.description: Late variety with large berries. Requires warmth.
Bogota.color_and_shape: Berries 30-50 g, elongated, dark red. Sweet, rich taste.
Bogota.farming: For southern regions. Low winter hardiness.

Bolshaya Medveditsa.color_and_shape: Berries 40-60 g, round-conical, red. Sweet flesh.

Borovitskaya.description: Old Russian variety, named after Borovitskaya Tower. Unpretentious.
Borovitskaya.color_and_shape: Berries 15-20 g, round, dark red. Classic sweet-sour taste.

Bravura.description: Early Italian variety. Characterized by uniform ripening.
Bravura.color_and_shape: Berries 25-35 g, conical, bright red, shiny. Sweet taste.

Brina.description: Day-neutral remontant variety. Productive, with dense berries.
Brina.color_and_shape: Berries 20-30 g, conical, red. Sweet-sour taste.

Bourbon.description: Variety with berries that have vanilla and caramel notes. Compact bush.
Bourbon.color_and_shape: Berries 25-35 g, round, dark red. Aromatic, sweet flesh.
Bourbon.taste: sweet with vanilla and caramel notes

Byche Serdtse.description: Large-fruited variety with heart-shaped berries. Named for its size.
Byche Serdtse.color_and_shape: Berries up to 80 g, heart-shaped, red. Somewhat loose flesh, sweet.

Bianca Fragola.description: White-fruited strawberry with wild strawberry aroma. Small-fruited, remontant.
Bianca Fragola.color_and_shape: Berries 5-8 g, round, white with red seeds. Very sweet.
Bianca Fragola.farming: Ornamental variety for pots. Low yield.

Victoria.description: Classic old English variety, standard for many gardeners. Unpretentious, winter-hardy.
Victoria.color_and_shape: Berries up to 50-75 g, glossy, sweet without sourness.
Victoria.farming: Bushes up to 40 cm. High yield. Propagates well by runners.

Gigantella.description: Dutch giant variety, impressive in berry and bush size. Requires a lot of space.
Gigantella.color_and_shape: Berries 40-60 g (up to 120 g), dark red, with pineapple flavor. Rough skin.
Gigantella.farming: Bush up to 50 cm tall and 60-70 cm in diameter. Frost-resistant to -25°C.

Darenka.description: Early non-remontant variety for universal use. Distinguished by outstanding winter hardiness, unpretentiousness, high stable yields. Has immunity to many diseases.
Darenka.color_and_shape: Dark red berries weighing ~14 g, heart-shaped with neck. Tender, sweet taste with light sourness.
Darenka.farming: Suitable for inexperienced gardeners. Requires regular watering without waterlogging. Plantation needs periodic renewal.

Dukat.description: Popular early Polish variety, consistently productive, excellent taste.
Dukat.color_and_shape: Berries 20-40 g (up to 60 g), round-conical, rich red, shiny. Red, juicy, sweet flesh.
Dukat.farming: Vigorous, compact bush. Yield 1-2 kg per bush.

Senga Sengana.description: German classic variety, very popular in Russia. Winter-hardy, disease resistant.
Senga Sengana.color_and_shape: Berries 20-30 g, dark red, shiny, broad cone. Sweet with sourness taste.
Senga Sengana.farming: Vigorous, compact bush, few runners. Yield up to 1 kg per bush.

Kent.description: Reliable early Canadian variety, high-yielding, winter-hardy.
Kent.maturity: Early, non-remontant. Ripens 6-10 days earlier than Senga Sengana.
Kent.color_and_shape: Berries 35-40 g, wide-conical or heart-shaped, intensely red with shine.
Kent.farming: Medium-sized bush. Medium winter hardiness. Resistant to powdery mildew, gray mold, mites. Store up to 10 days.
Kent.disease_resistance: resistant to powdery mildew, gray mold, mites

Clery.description: Early Italian variety. Medium-sized berries, conical shape, dark red color. Sweet taste with light sourness. Resistant to diseases and transport. Large, rich dark green, shiny leaves.
Clery.color_and_shape: Dark red berries weighing 25-45 g, regular conical shape, no voids. Dense, juicy flesh.
Clery.farming: Suitable for open ground and greenhouses. Tolerates short-term drought. Produces many runners, requires removal.

Lord.description: Famous English variety, one of the largest. Vigorous bush, many runners.
Lord.color_and_shape: Berries up to 60-100 g, round-conical, dark red. Dense, sweet, aromatic flesh.
Lord.farming: Winter-hardy. Yield up to 3 kg per bush. Demanding of watering and fertilizing.

Mara des Bois.description: Famous French remontant variety with wild strawberry aroma. Compact bush, runnerless.
Mara des Bois.color_and_shape: Berries 15-20 g, round-conical, bright red. Tender, sweet, very aromatic flesh.
Mara des Bois.farming: Requires regular watering. Tolerates heat poorly. Ideal for desserts.

Murano.description: Mid-early remontant Italian variety. Large, bright red, shiny berries. Regular conical shape. Dense, aromatic flesh. Excellent transportability.
Murano.maturity: Early remontant. Harvest from early summer until frost. From flowering to harvest ~30 days.
Murano.color_and_shape: Large red shiny berries weighing 20-25 g, regular conical shape. Juicy, fleshy, dense, aromatic flesh.
Murano.farming: Rare in Russia. Unpretentious in care, suitable for beginners.

Olvia.description: Very early variety, one of the best for commercial growing. Resistant to return frosts.
Olvia.maturity: Very early, non-remontant. From mid-late May, fruits for about a month.
Olvia.color_and_shape: Berries 30-60 g, blunt-conical with neck. Dense, juicy, sweet-sour flesh.
Olvia.farming: Vigorous, semi-spreading bush. Winter-hardy, drought-resistant, resistant to fungal diseases.

Rubinovy Kulon.description: Mid-late variety. Large berries, dark ruby color with shine. Round-conical shape. Dessert taste, sweet with subtle sourness. Universal purpose.
Rubinovy Kulon.origin: Russia, All-Russian Research Institute of Genetics and Breeding of Fruit Plants named after I.V. Michurin, 1980
Rubinovy Kulon.maturity: Mid-late, non-remontant. Flowering early June, ripening early July. Uniform.
Rubinovy Kulon.color_and_shape: Berries up to 30 g, cherry-ruby, sometimes burgundy. Not damaged when picking.
Rubinovy Kulon.farming: Large rosettes, plant at 45 cm distance. Rows north-south. Neutral acidity soil.

San Andreas.description: One of the best remontant varieties, fruits in waves from spring until frost. Compact bushes.
San Andreas.color_and_shape: Berries 25-30 g (up to 70 g), conical, glossy. Sweet-sour taste.
San Andreas.farming: Yield ~1 kg per bush. Demanding of watering and fertilizing.

Festivalnaya.description: Classic Soviet variety, time-tested. Resistant to frost and diseases.
Festivalnaya.color_and_shape: Berries 20-30 g, blunt-conical, red with shine. Dense flesh, sweet-sour, aromatic.
Festivalnaya.farming: Unpretentious. Yield up to 1 kg per bush. Stores well.

Florence.description: English late-season variety. Large-fruited, productive, with excellent commercial qualities.
Florence.color_and_shape: Berries 30-50 g (individual up to 60 g), wide-conical, dark red, shiny. Dense, dark red, very aromatic flesh.
Florence.farming: Vigorous, tall bush. Produces moderate runners. High yield. Excellent transportability.
Florence.planting_distance: 40-50 cm between bushes, 60-80 cm between rows
Florence.growing_regions: all of Europe, central Russia, south, Far East
Florence.disease_resistance: high to Verticillium, Phytophthora root rot, powdery mildew, gray mold

Honeoye.description: Early American variety, time-tested. Known for powerful bushes and excellent transportability.
Honeoye.maturity: Early, non-remontant. Ripens 7-9 days earlier than Senga Sengana. Fruits for 2-3 weeks.
Honeoye.color_and_shape: Berries up to 30 g, dark red with shine, conical with neck.
Honeoye.farming: Compact, vigorous bushes. Yield up to 146 centners/ha.

Chamora Turusi.description: Japanese giant variety, famous for very large berries and exceptional taste.
Chamora Turusi.color_and_shape: First berries up to 140-160 g, average 80-130 g, round-comb-shaped. Dense, very sweet flesh with peach aftertaste.
Chamora Turusi.farming: Vigorous, spreading bush, 30-40 cm. High yield. Resistant to gray mold.

Chyorny Prints.description: Variety with unusual dark berry color. Can be grown in one place for up to 7 years.
Chyorny Prints.maturity: Mid-early, remontant. From late June until autumn.
Chyorny Prints.color_and_shape: Berries 40-50 g, truncated-conical, dark carmine (appear black). Dense flesh.

Elsanta.description: Dutch variety, benchmark of taste. Very sweet, popular in Europe.
Elsanta.color_and_shape: Berries 30-40 g, round-conical, bright red. Dense flesh, sweet, with strawberry aroma.
Elsanta.farming: Demanding of watering. Susceptible to gray mold in rainy weather. Medium transportability.
